channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-26T10:11:00.832Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk. Specific case and fatality counts require direct verification against the current WHO Disease Outbreak News (who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news) or Africa CDC epidemic intelligence report.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 11 reported cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,952 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 21), representing 29 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.description: Mozambique Anticipatory Action Framework for Cholera (2026) ReliefWeb

item.description: Doctor evacuated from Congo is ‘helpless’ watching colleagues die of Ebola The Washington Post

item.description: Cholera: 27 killed as Borno records over 2,700 suspected Cholera cases Daily Trust

item.title: UKHSA: Guidance: Carbon monoxide (CO): antenatal checks algorithm for midwives
item.description: An algorithm for midwives to help diagnose carbon monoxide poisoning in pregnant women and offer advice for pregnant women in the event of exposure.

item.title: NPR: Therapists are using AI to take notes. Is it a useful tool or a breach of trust?
item.description: New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.

item.title: Google News: Upcoming COCA Call: What Clinicians Should Know about Ebola Bundibugyo Virus - Avian Flu Diary
item.description: Upcoming COCA Call: What Clinicians Should Know about Ebola Bundibugyo Virus Avian Flu Diary

item.description: Public health hot topic: Hantavirus Islands' Weekly

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: How Kyle Diamantas defied expectations as he rose to lead the FDA
item.description: WASHINGTON — People in the food world didn’t know what to expect when the Trump administration appointed a little-known Florida attorney as the FDA’s top food official in 2025. They…

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: The Ebola outbreak will lead to devastating violence against women and girls
item.description: “Women and girls in eastern Congo will be harmed in predictable, named ways over the next 12 months,” write researchers Lindsay Stark and Ilana Seff.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: 8 former CDC directors: Reform PEPFAR, don’t dismantle it
item.description: A State Department plan scheduled to be implemented June 1 would strip the U.S. of decades of disease defense, write eight former CDC directors.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: An AI biotech CEO sets the record straight on AI drug development hype
item.description: CEO of BigHat Biosciences, which designs antibody therapies using machine-learning, on how AI can, and cannot, help in drug development.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: The innovation trap: How pharma weaponizes a word to extend monopolies
item.description: “Just because a company invests does not mean it has invented anything novel,” Tahir Amin and Rohit Malpani write in this excerpt from “Pharma Monopoly.”

item.title: Google News: Bird flu detected in Kathmandu - Khabarhub
item.description: Bird flu detected in Kathmandu Khabarhub

item.description: Feds slam ‘unacceptable’ icebreaker fuel plan The Mercury
